title: malus × arnoldiana
intro: Malus × arnoldiana, also known as Arnold's crabapple, is a hybrid tree species between Malus baccata and Malus toringoides. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects, making it useful in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Malus × arnoldiana include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and act as a natural diuretic, among other uses. The herbal preparations of Malus × arnoldiana typically involve making teas, infusions, or tinctures from its leaves, bark, or fruits, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
